reason: REMARK STILL MISIDENTIFIES 0-CURRENT AS 1-CURRENT: The remark (lines 164-175 of lecA1.tex) states 'the functional phi -> int phi(gamma(tau)) d-tau defines a 1-current on R^4.' This functional acts on 0-forms (scalar test functions phi in C_c^inf(R^4)), so it is a 0-current (= distribution), not a 1-current. The integration current of the worldline gamma IS a 1-current, but it acts on 1-forms omega by [gamma](omega) = int omega_beta(gamma(tau)) dot-gamma^beta(tau) d-tau -- note the contraction with dot-gamma^beta, which the phi-functional lacks. The round-1 challenge ch-0c285e49 was marked resolved with the clarification 'Each component T^{alpha beta} is obtained from this 1-current by contracting the integrand with p^alpha d-gamma^beta,' but the fundamental misidentification persists: the displayed functional phi -> int phi d-tau is NOT the 1-current. The 1-current is omega -> int omega(dot-gamma) d-tau. The text should either (a) define the actual 1-current correctly and then explain how T^{alpha beta} derives from it, or (b) avoid calling the phi-functional a 1-current.
